verses.text: A three-year famine drives David to seek God, who answers: it is for Saul's slaughter of the Gibeonites, whom Israel had sworn to spare. Old, hidden guilt can lie buried for years and still cry out. God remembers broken oaths, and will have the wrong set right.

A three-year famine drives David to seek God, who answers: it is for Saul's slaughter of the Gibeonites, whom Israel had sworn to spare. Old, hidden guilt can lie buried for years and still cry out. God remembers broken oaths, and will have the wrong set right.

David asks the Gibeonites how to make atonement, and they ask for seven of Saul's house to be given up to death. The breach of a sacred covenant demands a costly reckoning. Sin against the helpless and against a sworn word is no light thing in God's eyes.

David asks the Gibeonites how to make atonement, and they ask for seven of Saul's house to be given up to death. The breach of a sacred covenant demands a costly reckoning. Sin against the helpless and against a sworn word is no light thing in God's eyes.

David asks the Gibeonites how to make atonement, and they ask for seven of Saul's house to be given up to death. The breach of a sacred covenant demands a costly reckoning. Sin against the helpless and against a sworn word is no light thing in God's eyes.

David asks the Gibeonites how to make atonement, and they ask for seven of Saul's house to be given up to death. The breach of a sacred covenant demands a costly reckoning. Sin against the helpless and against a sworn word is no light thing in God's eyes.

David spares Mephibosheth for his oath to Jonathan, but hands over seven of Saul's line, who are put to death before the LORD. Even in judgment David keeps his covenant of love. Mercy and justice meet: a promise to a friend is honoured while an old guilt is answered.

David spares Mephibosheth for his oath to Jonathan, but hands over seven of Saul's line, who are put to death before the LORD. Even in judgment David keeps his covenant of love. Mercy and justice meet: a promise to a friend is honoured while an old guilt is answered.

David spares Mephibosheth for his oath to Jonathan, but hands over seven of Saul's line, who are put to death before the LORD. Even in judgment David keeps his covenant of love. Mercy and justice meet: a promise to a friend is honoured while an old guilt is answered.

Rizpah, mother of two of the slain, spreads sackcloth on a rock and guards the bodies day and night from birds and beasts, through the long months till the rains come. A mother's grief keeps a faithful, heartbreaking vigil. Love watches over its dead with stubborn tenderness.

Rizpah, mother of two of the slain, spreads sackcloth on a rock and guards the bodies day and night from birds and beasts, through the long months till the rains come. A mother's grief keeps a faithful, heartbreaking vigil. Love watches over its dead with stubborn tenderness.

Moved by her devotion, David gathers the bones of Saul, Jonathan, and the seven, and buries them honourably in their family tomb — 'and after that God was intreated for the land.' When wrong is acknowledged and the dead are honoured, heaven relents. Mercy follows true reckoning.

Moved by her devotion, David gathers the bones of Saul, Jonathan, and the seven, and buries them honourably in their family tomb — 'and after that God was intreated for the land.' When wrong is acknowledged and the dead are honoured, heaven relents. Mercy follows true reckoning.

In a later battle a giant nearly kills the weary David, but Abishai rescues him, and the men swear he shall go to war no more, lest 'the light of Israel' be quenched. Even the mightiest grow old and need others' strength. The people guard the king who once guarded them.

In a later battle a giant nearly kills the weary David, but Abishai rescues him, and the men swear he shall go to war no more, lest 'the light of Israel' be quenched. Even the mightiest grow old and need others' strength. The people guard the king who once guarded them.

More battles follow against the giants of Gath, the kin of Goliath, and one by one they fall to David's men. The monsters that once terrified Israel are now routinely overcome. What the captain began against Goliath, his servants finish; in God's strength, every giant comes down at last.
